name: 4th Street Chocolate Kit Kat Bars
recipeInstructions:
CRUST:
1. Line a 9 x 13-inch cake pan with parchment paper.
2. Melt milk chocolate in a double boiler.
3. Stir peanut butter into the melted chocolate.
4. Place crushed corn flakes in a bowl and stir the chocolate/peanut butter mixture over them, mixing well.
5. Spread the mixture evenly into the prepared pan and cool in the refrigerator.
MOUSSE:
1. Melt semisweet chocolate in a double boiler.
2. Cream butter and sugar in a mixer fitted with a paddle until light and fluffy.
3. Add sifted cocoa powder and mix well.
4. Scrape down the bowl and add melted chocolate, mixing.
5. Continue mixing and add egg yolks one at a time, beating well after each addition.
6. Fold in heavy cream that has been beaten to firm peak, mixing well to incorporate without white streaks.
7. Spread mousse over the crust and chill for about 2 hours until set.
GANACHE:
1. Melt chocolate in a double boiler.
2. Mix sugar and heavy cream in a small saucepan and bring to a slow boil over low heat.
3. Pour the cream over chocolate and stir with a whisk to incorporate, ensuring all chocolate is melted.
4. Strain the mixture to remove any pieces of unmelted chocolate.
5. Let cool for about 1 hour.
6. Pour ganache over the firm bars to coat and refrigerate for 1 hour to set.
Note: Cut into desired size bars (e.g., 1 x 4-1/2 inch cuts will yield about 2 dozen) using a sharp knife dipped in hot water. Place bars on a cooling rack over a clean baking sheet. If the bars are soft, place them back in the freezer until ready to coat with ganache.
